unit.note: server vitest 156 files / 1847 tests green (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local); +4 vs pre-D02 (1843) from the ws-replay-disk-fallback guard set. Client vitest 202 files / 1969 tests green (unaffected — no client/src production change; the only client/ file is a Playwright E2E spec). tsc --noEmit clean.

integration.note: server/src/terminal/ws-replay-disk-fallback.test.ts — 4 independent must-not-modify guards (author!=fixer) exercising the REAL ws-upgrade spawn->resolve ordering with a real PtyManager + real HeadlessMirror + real SnapshotStore: GUARD 1 (RED on pre-fix main -> GREEN) disk-snapshot replayed after pty death; GUARD 2a live-mirror-wins (ADR-092) stays GREEN; GUARD 2b done/launch_failed replay-only branch still reads disk; GUARD 2c the attach resolve/replay path never disposes the mirror (rule 21).

e2e.note: Guard 3 real-browser spec committed (client/e2e/flows/v0-9-6-disk-snapshot-replay.spec.ts, kill-pty-then-reattach -> disk history rendered in xterm). Not run here: an isolated-stack Playwright run is infeasible/flaky in this environment (operator's live prod stack holds :3847; task-create rejects the unknown projectId 400 without a seeded temp store; Windows real-ConPTY/WS/StrictMode flakiness). Real-app proof deferred to the orchestrator's seeded isolated run of Guard 3 + the batched post-merge :3847 kill-pty-then-reattach smoke (Guard 4).

surface_verification.justification: Browser-OBSERVABLE change (embedded-terminal disk-snapshot replay renders in the real xterm), so a real-browser Guard 3 spec exists (client/e2e/flows/v0-9-6-disk-snapshot-replay.spec.ts, committed, RED-on-pre-fix-main). An isolated-stack Playwright run was attempted but is genuinely infeasible/flaky in THIS environment: (1) the canonical Hono port :3847 is held by the operator's LIVE production stack — running the destructive spec (creates/launches/DELETEs a real-ConPTY task) against it would pollute the operator's real USERPROFILE sessions and violates the isolated-USERPROFILE mandate; a clean run needs alt ports + a temp USERPROFILE; (2) POST /api/external/tasks rejects the spec's unknown projectId with 400 (validateProjectIdOrError) unless the exact projects.json is seeded into the temp store; (3) real-ConPTY + WS-ready + StrictMode-first-WS-abort + xterm-buffer-read are documented-flaky on Windows. No browser run is fabricated and the operator's live stack is not touched. Behavioral proof = the DETERMINISTIC server Guard 1 (server/src/terminal/ws-replay-disk-fallback.test.ts) which drives the REAL ws-upgrade spawn->resolve ordering with a real PtyManager + real HeadlessMirror + real SnapshotStore — proven RED on pre-fix main (empty fresh-mirror shadows the persisted disk snapshot) then GREEN after the fix (disk history replayed) — plus fences 2a/2b/2c, all inside the full server suite 1847/1847. Real-app confirmation deferred to the committed Guard 3 spec (runs green in the orchestrator's seeded isolated stack) + the mandatory post-merge :3847 kill-pty-then-reattach smoke (Guard 4), orchestrator-run. F2 browser-verify N/A: no production frontend diff — the only client/ file is the E2E test spec; the shipped client bundle is byte-identical to origin/main (detector false-positive on 'client/e2e' + the 'server/src/' substring).

behaviors.behavior: AC1/F02 — with NO live pty, the WS re-attach resolves the persisted DISK snapshot BEFORE spawn() so the fresh empty mirror can no longer shadow it; terminal history is replayed after pty death (server restart / stop-session / idle reap / shell exit)
behaviors.evidence: server/src/terminal/ws-replay-disk-fallback.test.ts GUARD 1 — real PtyManager+HeadlessMirror+SnapshotStore, process A flushes a marker to disk then kills the pty; a FRESH PtyManager on the SAME SnapshotStore runs the real ws-upgrade order and the emitted replay_snapshot carries the disk marker. Proven RED on pre-fix main, GREEN after 3d53edc.

behaviors.behavior: AC5/Guard 2a — with a LIVE pty, the resolve path returns the live mirror (ADR-092 live-first precedence not broken by the fix)
behaviors.evidence: server/src/terminal/ws-replay-disk-fallback.test.ts GUARD 2a — a stale disk sentinel is written, a live pty emits a marker; the replay_snapshot carries the live marker and NOT the stale disk sentinel. Stays GREEN.

behaviors.behavior: Guard 2b — the done/launch_failed replay-only branch still reads the disk snapshot (unchanged by the fix)
behaviors.evidence: server/src/terminal/ws-replay-disk-fallback.test.ts GUARD 2b — task state=done, disk snapshot written; replay-only branch emits it and closes 1000. Stays GREEN.

behaviors.behavior: Guard 2c — the WS-attach resolve/replay path never calls HeadlessMirror.dispose() (CLAUDE.md DO-NOT rule 21)
behaviors.evidence: server/src/terminal/ws-replay-disk-fallback.test.ts GUARD 2c — dispose spy installed AFTER process A teardown; asserts the re-attach resolve/replay path does not dispose the mirror. Stays GREEN.

behaviors.behavior: AC3 no-regression — the full existing server + client suites stay green; no ADR/invariant (ADR-092 live-first, rule-21 no-dispose, envelope shape, done-branch) regressed
behaviors.evidence: server vitest 1847/1847 + client vitest 1969/1969 + tsc --noEmit clean in F0.

behaviors.behavior: AC4 footprint / bloat baseline — the fix is confined to ws-upgrade-handler.ts ordering (pty-manager untouched); net-zero LOC; guards split into new sibling files to avoid ratcheting the grandfathered baselines (v0-9-6-live-pty-replay.spec.ts 372 LOC)
behaviors.evidence: git diff origin/main..HEAD = 3 files (ws-upgrade-handler.ts +11/-11; new ws-replay-disk-fallback.test.ts; new v0-9-6-disk-snapshot-replay.spec.ts). shipwright_bloat_baseline.json not ratcheted.

behaviors.behavior: Guard 3 — real-browser kill-pty-then-reattach renders the disk history in the actual xterm
behaviors.evidence: client/e2e/flows/v0-9-6-disk-snapshot-replay.spec.ts committed (RED-on-pre-fix-main, SHIPWRIGHT_E2E_REQUIRE_STACK=1 hard-fail on missing stack). Executed in the orchestrator's seeded isolated stack + the post-merge :3847 smoke (Guard 4); not runnable in this finalize env (live prod stack on :3847 + unseeded temp store).

test_completeness.note: The Guard 3 real-browser E2E is an ADDITIONAL real-deployment confirmation of the disk-snapshot-replay behavior already proven here by the deterministic server Guard 1 (real ws-upgrade ordering), not a distinct untested behavior; it runs in the orchestrator's seeded isolated stack + the post-merge :3847 smoke (Guard 4).
